title: CI flakes with podman-login
body:
I hit podman-login flakes quite frequently in the passed which suggests to have a look at it and track down potential sources for the flakes.

[+1077s] [Fail] Podman login and logout [BeforeEach] podman login and logout with flag --authfile 
[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:58
[+1077s] 
[+1077s] [Fail] Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout 
[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:101
[+1077s] 
[+1077s] [Fail] Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with --tls-verify 
[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:157
[+1077s] 
[+1077s] [Fail] Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with flag --authfile 
[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:135
[+1077s] 
[+1077s] [Fail] Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout 
[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:105

[+1077s]

[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:105
[+1077s] 
[+1077s] [Fail] Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with --cert-dir 
[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:177
[+1077s] 
[+1077s] [Fail] Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with --cert-dir 
[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:177

[+1077s] /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go:177

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vrothberg Is this still an issue or can we close?
Haven't seen them in a while. Much of the code has changed.

As best I can tell, the common factor is:
```
Error: error authenticating creds for "localhost:5002": error creating new docker client: error loading registries: error loading registries configuration "test/registries.conf": open test/registries.conf: no such file or directory
```
The string `error creating new docker client` appears only in [vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/docker/docker_client.go](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/docker/docker_client.go), in two places: [CheckAuth](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/docker/docker_client.go#L291) and [SearchRegistry](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/docker/docker_client.go#L354).

Near I can tell, the string `test/registries.conf` is (possibly?) being set in [test/e2e/libpod_suite_test.go](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/test/e2e/libpod_suite_test.go), possibly as the environment variable `REGISTRIES_CONFIG_PATH`. What I can't tell is if `INTEGRATION_ROOT` is empty or if `p.TempDir` is set to `test`, so I can't follow the code flow. 

I've spent too long on this without getting anywhere; will trust that the above links will help someone track it down quickly.

Starting with “no such file or directory” being true as written, `INTEGRATION_ROOT` being empty is a good hypothesis.

I have very little experience with Ginkgo; reading https://onsi.github.io/ginkgo/#parallel-specs , ginkgo parallel tests apparently run in separate _processes_, not threads/goroutines, so https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b1cc781c68964dff3ee4a00ef7ce71f56ae69e7c/test/e2e/common_test.go#L103 runs in only one of the processes and does not affect the others; so, the test fails if it is run in one of the other processes.

If this absolutely untested guess were correct, a quick could be copying the above line to the https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b1cc781c68964dff3ee4a00ef7ce71f56ae69e7c/test/e2e/common_test.go#L154 function as well. A good fix would probably be different.

Still flaking. PR #6903 and #7024 this time.

#### in-podman fedora-31 fedora-31

  2020-07-15T08:03:14  [integration_test](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5680780816941056/logs/integration_test.log)
*   Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with flag --authfile
  *   [same]
*   Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with --tls-verify
*   Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with flag --authfile
*   Podman login and logout [It] podman login and logout with --tls-verify
  *   [same]
*   Podman run networking [It] podman run network expose duplicate host port results in error

Could someone ginkgo-savvy please look into this? If noone else responds, I'll try @mtrmac's suggestion later this week.
